steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content: An 18% decrease leaves $100\%-18\%=82\%$ of the previous mass each period. So the multiplier per period is $0.82$, not $0.18$.

steps.title: Write the exponential decay form
steps.content: For periodic decay, use $M=M_{0}\cdot b^{t/T}$, where $b$ is the per-period factor and $T$ is the period length in the same units as $t$. Here $b=0.82$ and $T=11.4$ minutes, so:$$M=100(0.82)^{t/11.4}$$

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A) $M=100(0.18)^{\frac{t}{11.4}}$: uses $0.18$ as the multiplier, which would mean 82% lost per period, not 18%. Eliminated.
  • B) $M=100(0.18)^{t+11.4}$: wrong base and the exponent $t+11.4$ is not the number of periods. Eliminated.
  • C) $M=100(0.82)^{\frac{t}{11.4}}$: correct base $0.82$ and exponent $t/11.4$ counts periods. Matches.
  • D) $M=100(0.82)^{t+11.4}$: correct base, but $t+11.4$ is not the number of periods. Eliminated.
That matches Choice C — $M=100(0.82)^{\frac{t}{11.4}}$.
